---
name: ui-designer
description: Design beautiful interfaces using Material You, Minimalism, Glassmorphism, Neo-Brutalism, Claymorphism, and modern hybrids. Expert in Tailwind CSS, color harmonics, component theming, and accessibility (WCAG).
---

# UI Designer Skill

Expert design guidance for creating aesthetically pleasing, user-centric interfaces. This skill focuses on visual and structural design intent before and during implementation.

## Core Capabilities

### 1. Color Palette Generation
Generate cohesive and harmonic color palettes tailored to the project's vibe.
- Deliverables: HEX codes, Tailwind config extensions, and CSS variables.
- Palettes: Pastel, dark luxury, or tonal Material You sets.

### 2. Component Theming
Establish robust theme systems (Light/Dark) through consistent design tokens.
- Define `--bg`, `--text`, `--accent`, and `--border` variables.
- Ensure unified states (hover, focus, active) across UI elements.

### 3. Accessibility Audits
Evaluate and refine interfaces for inclusivity and compliance.
- Focus: WCAG AA/AAA contrast ratios, semantic HTML, and intuitive navigation.
- Guidance: ARIA attributes, focus ring management, and screen-reader friendliness.

## Core Design Languages

### 1. Material You (M3)
- Key traits: large rounded corners, tonal palettes, expressive typography.
- Reference: [material-you.md](references/material-you.md)

### 2. Minimalism
- Key traits: generous spacing, typography hierarchy, neutral palettes.
- Reference: [minimalism.md](references/minimalism.md)

### 3. Glassmorphism
- Key traits: backdrop blur, thin borders, vibrant gradient backdrops.
- Reference: [glassmorphism.md](references/glassmorphism.md)

### 4. Neo-Brutalism
- Key traits: thick borders, hard shadows, vibrant contrast, bold typography.
- Reference: [neo-brutalism.md](references/neo-brutalism.md)

### 5. Claymorphism
- Key traits: soft 3D surfaces, inner shadows, oversized radii, playful pastels.
- Reference: [claymorphism.md](references/claymorphism.md)

### 6. M3 Pastel Glass (Hybrid)
- Key traits: pastel blue/deep blue pairings, rounded corners, smooth morphing states.
- Reference: [m3-pastel-glass.md](references/m3-pastel-glass.md)

### 7. Neo-M3 Hybrid (Wired/Verge)
- Key traits: high contrast, solid borders, hard shadows, rounded geometry, tonal accents.
- Reference: [neo-m3-hybrid.md](references/neo-m3-hybrid.md)

## VS Code Copilot Usage

Use this skill when prompting Copilot Chat for UI work.

### Example prompts
- Design this page in Material You style with a pastel palette and accessible contrast.
- Refactor this Angular component to Minimalism with a clear typography hierarchy.
- Apply Neo-M3 Hybrid styling while preserving responsive behavior and WCAG AA.

## Workflows

### 1. Design Conception
When starting a feature, define:
- Primary style: Material You, Minimalism, Glassmorphism, Neo-Brutalism, Claymorphism, M3 Pastel Glass, or Neo-M3 Hybrid.
- Palette vibe: Pastel, dark, or high-contrast.

### 2. Component Architecture
Plan HTML/Angular structure with Tailwind classes, responsive behavior, and accessibility-first semantics.

## Best Practices
- Consistency: stick to one main design language per feature area.
- Accessibility: ensure text and UI contrast meets WCAG AA at minimum.
- Pragmatism: keep design implementation lean and maintainable.
